qualifying_org_definition: Any organization where the ABAWD performs unpaid volunteer work, including nonprofits, government agencies, and in-kind work arrangements; Arizona policy does not appear to require a pre-approved list or MOU for the general volunteer lane (MOU/DES-assignment requirements apply only to the workfare lane, not the general volunteer lane). Multiple independent sources — DES public pages, LegalClarity, BenefitsUSA, and USDA federal policy — confirm that volunteering 'at a food pantry, library, park, or other nonprofit or public service organization counts' without a pre-approved site list.
verification_instrument: No specific state-issued form number identified for nonprofit volunteer hour certification. Arizona requires third-party documentation (effective September 2, 2025, self-statements are no longer accepted); acceptable documentation is a written statement/letter from the volunteer organization confirming the number of hours worked. FAA-1530A covers ABAWD time-limit notices; FAA-1786A is the ABAWD/Work Registration Script; neither is a volunteer-hours certification form issued to nonprofits. The standard is: org-signed letter or statement containing hours worked.
hours_formula_detail: Arizona follows the federal standard of 80 hours per month (approximately 20 hours per week); confirmed by DES public pages, Propel guide, and multiple independent sources.
accepts_outofstate_detail: No Arizona DES policy source explicitly addresses whether a certifying nonprofit must be incorporated or registered in Arizona. The policy language consistently refers to 'any organization' where the ABAWD performs unpaid volunteer work, without a location or registration qualifier. Under federal default (7 CFR 273.24), any qualifying nonprofit may serve as a volunteer site regardless of state of incorporation; absent a state-level restriction, out-of-state nonprofits such as a CA-based 501(c)(3) should be operationally accepted. No targeted search (AZ SNAP ABAWD nonprofit registration in-state, AZ volunteer organization location requirement) returned any contradicting restriction.
geographic_waivers: None as of mid-2026. Prior to the One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA), Arizona held a partial geographic waiver covering tribal reservation areas and most non-Maricopa counties under the 'insufficient jobs' criteria. OBBBA terminated or superseded all existing ABAWD geographic waivers; as of early 2026 the FNA listed no active ABAWD time-limit waivers for any state. Arizona is now fully enforced statewide under the expanded OBBBA rules (ages 18–64).
submission_deadline: By the 10th day of the month following the month the change occurs (per DES change-reporting rules: 'You must report any changes by the 10th day of the month following the month the change occurs').
notes:
Arizona (state-administered via DES) allows volunteer work to count directly toward the 80-hour monthly ABAWD requirement without a mandatory prestep like an EDP, county assignment, or MOU — those requirements apply only to the workfare lane (DES-assigned hours). The general volunteer lane is self-directed. As of September 2, 2025, DES no longer accepts client self-statements; org-signed third-party documentation (e.g., a letter from the volunteer organization) is required, but no specific state-issued form number for nonprofit certification of volunteer hours was found (confidence: medium). The policy manual at dbmefaapolicy.azdes.gov returned 403 errors and could not be directly verified.

REFINEMENT PASS (2026-06-21): Adversarial research conducted across four angles — work registration as blocking prestep, E&T State Plan volunteer-track gating, approved site list existence, and legal aid sources — failed to identify any hidden prestep that would undermine the Tier 1 rating. Key findings: (1) Work registration under 7 CFR 273.7 is a general SNAP administrative requirement (register for employment assistance at application) that is explicitly distinguished from ABAWD qualifying activities under 7 CFR 273.24; multiple sources confirm volunteer hours satisfy the ABAWD requirement independently without a prior assignment. (2) No Arizona-specific approved volunteer site list or MOU requirement was found in any source; BenefitsUSA and LegalClarity both confirm any qualifying volunteer work at a nonprofit, food pantry, library, or public organization counts. (3) Employment Career Development Plans (ECDPs) appear only in the SNAP CAN mandatory E&T program context — not as a prestep for ABAWDs self-selecting the volunteer lane. (4) The AZ DES SNAP E&T State Plan PDFs (FFY2020, FFY2021, and the most recent unlabeled plan) all 403'd; however, the SNAP CAN page at des.az.gov/snapcan-training confirms E&T is a separate program from the general volunteer qualifying lane. (5) county_variation_flag updated to TRUE: prior notes referenced a Maricopa County waiver; post-OBBBA research clarifies the prior waiver covered tribal reservation areas and non-Maricopa counties under 'insufficient jobs'; Maricopa itself was not waived. All waivers terminated under OBBBA. (6) Verification instrument remains 'org-signed letter with hours worked' — no specific AZ form number found despite targeted form-library searches (FAA-1701A is an authorization-to-release form; FAA-1573A is a language-access form; neither is a volunteer hours certification form). Remote stance remains unknown — no sources addressed remote volunteer work specifically. Confidence raised to HIGH: the volunteer lane, no-prestep finding, and org-letter verification are confirmed across at least 5 independent sources including DES public pages, LegalClarity, BenefitsUSA, CLSAZ, and federal FNA guidance. The only unresolved material field is remote_stance, which does not affect Tier classification.

EXTENDED FIELDS PASS (2026-06-21): (1) Hours formula: AZ follows federal 80 hours/month baseline — confirmed by DES public pages and multiple sources; hours_formula=federal_80. (2) Out-of-state nonprofit: policy is SILENT — no AZ source restricts volunteer orgs by location or in-state registration; federal default (any qualifying nonprofit) applies; out-of-state 501(c)(3)s like a CA-based org should be operationally accepted. (3) Geographic waivers: all prior AZ waivers (tribal reservation areas, non-Maricopa counties under 'insufficient jobs') were terminated under OBBBA; FNA listed no active AZ waivers as of early 2026; AZ is fully enforced statewide. county_variation_flag remains true as a historical note but waivers are no longer active. (4) Submission UX: recipients report via MyFamilyBenefits online portal, fax (844-680-9840), or in-person at county DES office; reporting is monthly with a deadline of the 10th day of the following month per DES change-reporting rules. (5) Form required fields: no specific AZ form exists; the standard org-signed letter baseline applies.
